mappings.notes: Unintended exposure of training data or sensitive context through model outputs
Extraction of confidential information via targeted model queries
Reconstructing training data from model outputs or confidence scores
Organisational policies for data privacy in AI systems — classification, handling, and disclosure controls
Sensitive information disclosure categorised and risk-rated in AI system risk register
Evaluation programme includes data leakage and privacy disclosure testing
Defined procedures for responding to sensitive data disclosure incidents

mappings.control_id: Training data for high-risk AI must be relevant, representative, free of errors, and complete — must address privacy
mappings.notes: Privacy-preserving training data practices are a compliance requirement for high-risk LLMs

mappings.control_id: Users must receive information about capabilities, limitations, and conditions of use
mappings.notes: Disclosure of potential data disclosure risks is a transparency obligation

mappings.control_id: Documented quality management system including post-market monitoring
mappings.notes: Post-market monitoring must detect and respond to sensitive disclosure incidents

mappings.control_id: GPAI providers must maintain technical documentation including training data governance
mappings.notes: Data governance for GPAI training data is a binding documentation obligation from Aug 2025
Output redaction for PII and sensitive patterns before responses reach users
DLP on all LLM output channels — API, chat interface, logs
All data in LLM scope classified — training data, RAG sources, outputs, embeddings
Access controls on RAG retrieval — users retrieve only data they are authorised to access
Training data and RAG content quality requirements — sensitivity, completeness, appropriateness assessed before use
Data provenance documented — source, classification, handling requirements tracked from ingestion through all derived forms
Output scanning and redaction as AIMS security controls — DLP enforced at the system boundary
Impact assessment covers data disclosure risk — what data is in LLM scope, what is the disclosure impact per stakeholder

mappings.control_id: 3.1 Establish and maintain data management process
mappings.notes: Data classification and handling requirements applied to all LLM data assets
Embeddings, training data, and RAG caches containing sensitive data encrypted
User training on LLM data handling — what data should not be shared with LLMs
Detailed logging of all LLM data access — RAG queries, data retrieved, outputs generated

mappings.control_name: Verify sensitive data is not cached or exposed in logs
mappings.notes: LLM outputs containing sensitive data not logged in cleartext or cached without protection

mappings.control_name: Verify PII is identified and protected
mappings.notes: PII in LLM training data, RAG sources, and outputs identified and handled per policy

mappings.control_name: Verify access control decisions enforce least privilege
mappings.notes: RAG retrieval access controls — users retrieve only data they are authorised to access

mappings.control_name: Verify all sensitive data encrypted at rest
mappings.notes: Training data, embeddings, RAG document stores, and prompt caches encrypted at rest

mappings.control_name: Verify all sensitive data encrypted in transit
mappings.notes: All LLM API communication and data flows encrypted in transit — TLS 1.2 minimum
All OT data accessed by LLMs encrypted in transit — no cleartext historian queries over OT network
OT data used in LLM context or stored by LLM components encrypted at rest
LLM outputs containing OT data restricted to authorised users — no unrestricted external output
All LLM access to OT data systems requires authenticated, authorised identity
LLMs with historian access as a new vector for automated OT intelligence gathering
OT data classification applied to all data accessible by LLMs

mappings.control_id: Protecting OT data at rest and in transit
mappings.notes: Encryption and access controls on all OT data paths feeding LLM context
Application
OT data used in LLM context encrypted at rest — historian exports, embedding stores, prompt caches
LLM access to OT data enforced by classification — sensitive process and network data requires elevated access tier
LLM access logs to OT data protected — audit trail of all OT data accessed by LLM
Data-at-rest protected — training data, embeddings, RAG stores containing sensitive information encrypted
Data-in-transit protected — all LLM API calls and RAG retrieval paths encrypted
LLM output channels monitored for sensitive data patterns — DLP coverage
Risk tolerance established — acceptable levels of sensitive data disclosure risk defined per use case

mappings.control_id: Policy identifying confidential information in LLM scope and how it is handled — training data, RAG corpus, outputs
Technical controls protecting confidential data in LLM pipelines — encryption, access controls, output scanning
Personal information in LLM scope identified — training data, RAG sources, outputs — collection documented
Personal information used only for purposes disclosed — LLM processing of customer PII limited to agreed use cases
Access controls on RAG data sources — users retrieve only data they are authorised to access

mappings.control_name: Protect stored account data — SAD prohibition
mappings.notes: SAD (CVV, PIN) must never be in LLM training data or RAG — stored after authorisation is prohibited

mappings.control_name: Protect stored account data — PAN rendering
mappings.notes: PANs in LLM outputs masked — only first six and last four digits displayed in any LLM response
Any PAN in LLM scope encrypted with strong cryptography — AES-256 or equivalent
All LLM API calls and RAG retrieval paths carrying CHD encrypted in transit — TLS 1.2 minimum

mappings.control_name: Restrict access by need to know
mappings.notes: LLM access to CHD restricted to minimum required — retrieval access controls prevent over-broad PAN access
All data in LLM scope classified and governed — training corpora, RAG sources, embeddings, outputs
DLP on all LLM output channels — AI-specific monitoring covering sensitive data patterns
GDPR and EU AI Act Article 10 data governance obligations addressed for LLM deployments
Training data, embeddings, and RAG stores encrypted at rest and in transit
Data classification and handling requirements defined for all LLM-integrated applications before development
Output redaction and DLP implemented in code — LLM output scanned before delivery, reviewed in build
Data protection requirements verified in testing — PII patterns confirmed not to appear in LLM outputs
Production DLP monitoring on LLM output channels — operational data protection as ongoing practice

mappings.control_id: PII and sensitive data extraction from outputs
mappings.notes: Attempt to extract PII, credentials, financial data, and confidential content from model outputs through direct questions, social engineering prompts, and adversarial extraction techniques
Verify that DLP controls on LLM outputs correctly detect and block sensitive data patterns before delivery
Verify that RAG retrieval and context population enforce user authorisation — users cannot access documents above their permission level

mappings.control_name: PII protection and data leakage prevention
mappings.notes: Foundational
Foundational
Foundational

mappings.control_id: LLM outputs containing API keys, tokens, or credentials
mappings.notes: Output filtering: detect and redact credential patterns in all outputs

mappings.control_id: Credentials in plaintext config files ingested by LLM
mappings.notes: Audit all config and data sources for embedded credentials

mappings.control_name: Secure coding — ML pipeline output handling
mappings.notes: Implement secure coding practices for all code paths that consume LLM output; treat model responses as untrusted input to downstream systems

mappings.control_name: Review for security vulnerabilities — output validation
mappings.notes: Include output injection and unsafe rendering scenarios in pre-release security reviews; verify that output sanitisation controls are present and effective

mappings.control_name: Verify software integrity — model artifact integrity
mappings.notes: Verify that the model artifact serving output has not been tampered with; maintain signed model checksums and verify before deployment

mappings.control_name: Test for security vulnerabilities — output security
mappings.notes: Include output-based injection testing (XSS, SQLi, CMDi via LLM output) in adversarial test suites

mappings.control_name: Protection of Information at Rest — training data and model weights
mappings.notes: Encrypt training data, fine-tuning datasets, and model weights at rest; prevent data memorisation exposure through access controls on data stores
Log all model inference requests and responses with sufficient detail to detect sensitive data disclosure; include user identity, query content, and response metadata
Enforce role-based access control on model inference endpoints; restrict access to training data, fine-tuning data, and model configuration based on clearance and need-to-know
Monitor model outputs for sensitive data patterns — PII, credentials, classification markings — and alert on detection

mappings.control_name: Protection and Prevention — data disclosure controls
mappings.notes: Implement ICT security controls to prevent LLM-based disclosure of sensitive financial data, PII, and credentials; include output monitoring and data loss prevention

mappings.control_name: ICT Incident Management — data breach reporting
mappings.notes: Classify LLM data disclosure events as ICT-related incidents; report to competent authorities per DORA incident classification and reporting requirements

mappings.control_name: ICT Risk Management — AI data governance
mappings.notes: Include AI data handling in ICT risk management framework; define policies for data processed by LLM systems including classification, retention, and access controls
Deploy detection mechanisms for sensitive data in model outputs; monitor for PII, financial data, and credential patterns in inference responses

incidents.name: Samsung employees leak source code and meeting notes via ChatGPT
OpenAI Redis caching bug exposes user conversation history
GitHub Copilot reproduces verbatim licensed code and embedded secrets
Slack AI indirect injection via channel content
DeepSeek AI database exposure — 1M+ chat logs publicly accessible
Cursor AI code agent leaking repository secrets via context window
